description: The spec defines activeFilterTags as a FeedViewState field (section 6.1) and expects filter state to persist across operations (EC-017: 'Active filter tags persist across note switches'). However, the existing feedReducer.ts pattern (line 26-49) replaces the entire FeedViewState on each DomainSnapshotReceived action — it destructures snapshot fields into a brand-new state object without preserving any local-only UI state like activeFilterTags. The spec does not address how activeFilterTags survives the snapshot-replacement architecture. If the reducer overwrites activeFilterTags on every snapshot, the filter state would be lost on any domain event (save, delete, edit).

secondEvidence.snippet: activeFilterTags: readonly string[];      // currently selected filter tags
